data.title: Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. GAAP EPS of $0.40 misses by $0.73, revenue of $262M beats by $4.46M
data.description:
• Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. press release (PXED): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.40 misses by $0.73.
  • Revenue of $262M (+2.9% Y/Y) beats by $4.46M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA was $75.2 million for first quarter 2026, compared to $70.1 million for first quarter 2025, and adjusted diluted earnings per share was $1.38 for the first quarter 2026, compared to $1.35 for first quarter 2025.2

    As of November 30, 2025, the Company’s cash and cash equivalents (including restricted cash and cash equivalents) and marketable securities (including current and noncurrent marketable securities) totaled $218.1 million, compared to $194.8 million as of August 31, 2025. The increase was primarily attributable to $31.1 million of cash generated by operating activities, which was partially offset by $4.7 million of capital expenditures.

data.title: ICF secures $300 million in new European contracts
data.description:
• ICF (ICFI) said on Tuesday that it has secured two significant new contracts with European institutions to design and deliver large-scale public communication campaigns across all 27 European Union (EU) Member States. 
  • The contracts, one single-award and one multiple-award, were secured in the third and fourth quarters of 2025. They have a combined ceiling value of nearly $300M with a period of performance of up to five years.
  • The integrated citizen engagement campaigns will make EU priorities clear, relatable, and relevant to audiences, the company said. "ICF will tap into its extensive European network of agencies, local correspondents, and content creators to implement the campaign and leverage advanced, AI-enabled technology to monitor sentiment, counter misinformation, and optimize performance in real time."
  • ICFI +0.11% after hours to $93.06.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wall Street ends in the red as retail inflation comes in slightly cooler, earnings fail to lift sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages finished lower on Tuesday, as investors assessed cooler-than-expected core CPI inflation and some important earnings reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.2% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.8%.

“Sellers sent the major indices lower in the morning. By noon, buyers lifted the index, erasing earlier losses, only for sellers to take the index slightly lower. Geopolitically, Trump’s ‘Don-roe Doctrine’ is in focus relating to Venezuela and Iran. This gave energy firms like ExxonMobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) a lift today,” Chris Lau, Investing Group Leader for DIY Value Investing, told Seeking Alpha.

“Overall, stock markets could not shake off two headwinds: inflation and credit card regulation. Trump did not want consumers to face higher electricity bills from AI data centers in the former, and he wanted a 10% cap on swipe fees in the latter case,” Lau added.

The December Consumer Price Index came in at +0.3% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.7% YoY vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.6% YoY vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

In other news, President Donald Trump said he'll announce his nomination for the Federal Reserve chair within "the next few weeks," as the Justice Department continues probe into current Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Also, new home sales slipped 0.1% MoM to 737K in October, vs. 716K expected, from 738K in the prior month, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

Among companies reporting on Tuesday, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) -4% posted better-than-expected earnings, while Delta Air Lines (DAL) -2.4% had year-over-year revenue growth. 

Big companies reporting this week include Bank of America (BAC), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Citigroup (C).

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.18%, while the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was 1 basis point lower to 3.53%. A 30-Year bond auction traded through.

data.title: Park Aerospace GAAP EPS of $0.15, revenue of $17.33M

data.title: Netflix considering changing bid for Warner Bros. to all cash - report
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) is working on revised terms for it Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) purchase and is considering making its offer all cash for the company's studios and streaming businesses.

The changes are part of an effort to speed up a sale that's expected to take months to close and comes as rival bidder Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has offered an all-cash $30 a share, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Warner Bros. rose 1.1%, while Netflix gained 1.3%.

Developing story ...

data.title: Burford Capital announces pricing and upsizing of private offering of senior notes
data.description:
• Burford Capital (BUR) on Tuesday announced the pricing of its private offering of $500 million aggregate principal amount of 8.500% senior notes due 2034 by its indirect, wholly owned subsidiary, Burford Capital Global Finance, which represents an increase from the previously announced offering size.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 15, 2026.
  • Burford Capital intends to use the net proceeds from the offering of the Securities to redeem as soon as practicable following the closing of the offering the 5.000% bonds due 2026 of Burford Capital and the remainder for general corporate purposes, which may include the repayment or retirement of other existing indebtedness.
  • BUR is -0.72% to $9.57.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kodiak Gas Services raised at Barclays, as execution plus value equals opportunity
data.description:
Kodiak Gas Services (KGS) +2.6% in Tuesday's trading as Barclays upgraded the operator of contract compression infrastructure for oil and gas industry customers to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $42 price target, raised from $35, citing a positive growth outlook, strong execution track record, and discounted valuation.

Kodiak (KGS) has a strong fundamental story in its asset composition and geographical reach as well as industry-leading mechanical uptime and fleet utilization, and continues to see tailwinds from a resilient demand outlook for compression services amid supply tightness with extended lead times for new orders, Barclays analyst Theresa Chen said.

Compression remains a key part of the value chain that enables gas to move efficiently from the wellhead to end markets, but extended lead times of 80 or more weeks for new compression equipment have created tightness between supply and demand, and Chen believes this constrained supply underpins steady and durable demand as well as pricing power for Kodiak's (KGS) compression services, which are essential in helping producers bring gas volumes to market reliably and efficiently.

EQT's (EQT) full exit in December has removed any risk related to sponsor overhang, Chen said, expecting greater investor receptivity for Kodiak (KGS) in 2026 with large sell-downs no longer on the horizon, and the stock should trade more in line with its fundamental profile, with one less key risk.

data.title: Unity Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.52
data.description:
• Unity Bancorp press release (UNTY): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.52.
